title: vitex buchananii var. quadrangula
intro: Vitex buchananii var. quadrangula, also known as the Quadangular Vitex or Quadangular Chaste Tree, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, which is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating menstrual disorders, menopausal symptoms, and other gynecological issues. Its extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogen-modulating properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps, anxiety, and hot flashes. Vitex buchananii var. quadrangula can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbal remedies to enhance its therapeutic effects.
